Ibani: A bịtẹ sịghịrị fịnịm dedebie, na ogosi mingi birigha nye
English: I finished washing clothes just now since morning, that is why I haven't bathed.

Ibani: Daḅọ ḅẹm ḅẹ, ị ịḅarana mingi pa wa sịghịrị pịghị wamina (oju)birima
English: Father said that you should bathe us yourself and dress us up.
Do you want to lie down here?
Yes, I want to lie down here.

Ibani: Ofori o, A minga ḅụlọlọ dọụgha
English: No, I don't want to lie down here.

Ibani: Arị ma ani mịnga ḅụlọlọarị ḅọ saghị ene-ene
English: She has been the one lying down here since.
You (plural) should go and lie down and sleep.

Ibani: Enegbọrụ nnị ibila ma ḅụ ḅụlọlọm
English: They lay down on the mat all day.

Ibani: Ene gonyegonye, nnị ibila ma ḅụ ḅụlọlọ ḅụlọlọm
English: Everyday, they lie down on the mat.

Ibani: Ị ibila ma ḅụ ḅụlọlọ bẹ?
English: Will you lie down on the mat?

Ibani: Ịịn A ibila ma ḅụ ḅụlọlọ bẹm
English: Yes I will lie down on the mat.

Ibani: Wa ibila ma ḅụ ḅụlọlọ bịgha
English: We will not lie down on the mat.
Didn't you lie down on the mat?
I didn't lie down on the mat.
Do not lie down there again.

Ibani: Pọọsị ma na anị awọ na munoarị
English: The cat and its kitten are sleeping.
They want to sleep in their room.
When are you going to sleep?

Ibani: A esenie enekịẹnye ḅụ mu muno bẹm
English: I am going to sleep by 9 o'clock.
I will sleep in your house.

Ibani: Ị tego sị saa mamgba muno muno?
English: Why do you sleep all the time?

Ibani: Ị ḅu kụrọagh anị A go saa mamgba muno muno nye
English: I'm not well, that's why I'm sleeping all the time.

Ibani: A ḅụlọlọ saa muno ịtọrụ fafaam
English: Whenever I lie down sleep disappears from my eyes.
Where did she sleep last night?

Ibani: Á warị bie munom bịa-ene dii
English: She slept in the house last night.
He is the Lord of praise.
I am going to the market.
What do you want to buy?

Ibani: A fụlọ sọọ nye fẹ dọghụm
English: I want to buy items for cooking soup.

Ibani: Ị tẹnga fẹghẹ mu n'fẹ dọghụ?
English: Which market do you want to go to?

Ibani: A Okoloma fẹghẹ mu n'fẹ dọghụm
English: I want to go and buy them from Bonny main market.

Ibani: A nama gbogbo fẹghẹ mu n'fẹ bẹm
English: I am going to buy them from slaughter market.

Ibani: Je fẹghẹ mu sị mu n'fẹ
English: Go and buy them from another market.

Ibani: A samuno nji na ḅuru na deriari
English: I am selling dry fish and yam.

Ibani: Ị tọrọpa nji ma na ḅuru ma na dẹrịari?
English: How are you selling the fish and the yam?

Ibani: Nji ma Naịra sọnọ-a-ndẹ, kuma ḅuru ma Naịra mmẹ-a-ndẹ oyi-a-si findi
English: The fish is ₦2k, while the yam is ₦1k.

Ibani: Ḅọrọbẹ a ₦1500 pa ị'gbẹẹ nji ma ḅụ, pịị ₦700 pa i'gbẹẹ ḅuru ma bu
English: What if I pay you ₦1500 for the fish, and ₦700 for the yam.

Ibani: Ofori o, anị laa (kara) bịgha, nye nyelaa ḅaam
English: No oh, it will not be enough, things are very expensive.

Ibani: A ine nji ma pa ịpịrị ḅẹm ₦1,800, pịghị ḅuru ma pa ịpịrị ₦800
English: I can give you the fish for ₦1800 and the yam for ₦800.

Ibani: Bẹrẹ ofori, nnị pa akpa ma bie sụa ọkọm
English: Okay, put them in the bag for me.
I will put them for you.

Ibani: Paịsịsị, iye acct no sịị ọkọm
English: Please call your account number for me.
I have sent money to you.

Ibani: Bẹrẹ ofori, A n'sụa ịpịrị ḅẹm
English: Ok, I will put them for you.

Ibani: A iye igbi fịrịma ị'pịrị mam
English: I have sent you, your money.

Ibani: Paịsịsị ḅarasụa ịpịrị wa nwabite ma ḅụ mingi ma imima sii
English: Please join me to squeeze out water from this wrapper.

Ibani: Ị munga munga, ḅuru tumuni tarịarị, tẹgosi?
English: Anywhere you go, people are hungry. Why?

Ibani: Ị dughonye gonye gonye anị Ịkpụrụma bẹm, anị goo ịbịpị gbẹẹ
English: Anything you say will be used against you, so better keep quiet.
Anybody is not everybody, don't blame him.

Ibani: ọmịna bie, nnị ine gbereḅọ sẹlẹakị bem
English: Anyone of you people can be chosen.

Ibani: Isiapụ ma na kpọrọfịnị opu ịkasị ma ọgọnọ
English: Sit with others on the table.

Ibani: Sotọrụ ma ọfịrịm, A imo suarị
English: The weather is hot, I am sweating.

Ibani: Ọ yọ ma fiema ma mịnga
Tumini ma furuapụ ma sịbị silem
English: The people hoot at the criminal.

Ibani: Mie bịtẹ ma olom mam, paịsịsị anị pa ḅumaa
English: This cloth is old, please discard it.

Ibani: Oḅori ma fimaa sị anị wangịma ḅọrọ
English: Untie the goat to move about.

Ibani: Ḅuru ma Kpọọ gboloma, nnị ine anị dighi ḅara
English: Tie the yams together so that they can be carried.

Ibani: A ojukụnụmanye ma Kụnụ ḅẹlẹ mam
English: I love the fragrance of this perfume.
You are pressing me tight, please stop.

Ibani: Wamina Toru findi, ama egere ibi gboloma nye ḅụ
English: Enlighten us more about the importance of native language.

Ibani: Ị tọrụ pụgha, A ine goo igbi ma arị ḅịghanye
English: My eyes are not blind that I cannot see the money.

Ibani: Ḅọghụ sị akị, anị wa kọọ tii̇ wamịna ịrụ saa
English: Hide and take was the game we played when we were young.
